message:
refactor(macros,skeleton): let-chain style, skeleton_placeholder!(), notify bug reproduction test

asupersync-macros/src/scope.rs: Flatten nested `if let` + `if matches!` into a
single let-chain guard (`if let Stmt::Expr(expr, _) = stmt && matches!(...)`)
using Rust 1.87 let-chains. Reduces nesting by one level, no behavioral change.

asupersync-macros/src/session.rs: Same let-chain refactoring for the msg-type
parsing branch in ProtocolDef::parse. Flattens `if let Ok(ident)` + `if ident
== "msg"` into a single chained condition.

asupersync_v4_api_skeleton.rs: Replace every todo!() call (dozens across Cx,
Scope, handle, channel, actor, remote, runtime, lab, and macro stubs) with a
new domain-specific `skeleton_placeholder!()` macro that panics with an
actionable message directing users to the src/ runtime implementation. This
replaces generic "not yet implemented" panics with clear guidance. Also
normalizes formatting to multi-line rustfmt style throughout.

src/sync/notify_bug_test.rs: Rewrite the test from a passing regression test
for baton-pass behavior into a bug-reproduction test for a subtle
notify_one/notify_waiters duplicate-wakeup interaction. The new test
demonstrates how: (1) two waiters register, (2) notify_one targets fut1,
(3) notify_waiters increments generation, (4) fut1's baton-pass leaks the
notify_one permit as a spurious stored notification that could incorrectly
wake a future created after all notifications. All waiter_count() assertions
removed in favor of behavioral assertions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
